provenanceJourney.event: Recovered in fishing nets in the Adriatic
Moved through dealers; leaves Italy without an export licence
Acquired by the J. Paul Getty Museum
Italian Court of Cassation upholds the forfeiture order
ECHR rejects the Getty's challenge to the confiscation

redFlags.evidence: Netted by Italian fishermen in the Adriatic in 1964 and moved out of Italy without an export licence; Italian prosecutions followed over its handling.
Italian courts ordered forfeiture and the Court of Cassation upheld it; in 2024 the European Court of Human Rights rejected the Getty's challenge to the confiscation order.
Italy — a UNESCO 1970 source country with an enforceable domestic confiscation order outstanding.

repatriation.note: Italy's confiscation order stands after the 2024 ECHR ruling. The Getty disputes Italian jurisdiction over an object it says was acquired in international waters and outside Italy, and the bronze remains at the Getty Villa.

sources.title: Carabinieri TPC — illicitly removed cultural property

registryChecks.registry: INTERPOL Stolen Works of Art Database
registryChecks.method: Domain-scoped search of interpol.int. This searches the registry's public WEBSITE, not its register.
registryChecks.caveat: No evidence surfaced through the access available. This is NOT a statement that the object is absent from this register, and it is NOT a clean bill of health — objects looted at source were never inventoried and cannot appear in a stolen-property register at all.

registryChecks.method: No programmatic interface exists. Deep link emitted for a human to run the authoritative search.
registryChecks.caveat: This register could not be searched programmatically. Nothing about the object has been established either way — the official search below must be run by a human.

registryChecks.method: SPARQL over Q642140 (Victorious Youth) — significant-event (P793), collection (P195), origin (P495), discovery (P189). Chosen from 2 label-search candidate(s): Q642140 Victorious Youth; Q66564581 Victorious Youth. Entity resolution is shown so the choice can be audited rather than trusted.
